author: bharath.vedartham

author: bharath.vedartham
content: things were much better during that time, i m not sure why grandine reorged 11 blocks out

author: parithosh
content:
```
  The chain split at slot 22688 because Gloas / ePBS envelope propagation broke — the new layer where the beacon block and its execution payload are gossiped as separate objects. Consensus rules, fork choice, and
   EL block-processing did not break first; they failed downstream. The chain ran for hours on the proposers whose envelope path still worked, then stopped at slot 23229 when that small set ran out of duty slots.

  Most likely single defect

  Gossip topic-ID disagreement between CL clients on the new ePBS topics — execution_payload_envelope and payload_attestation_message. One bug explains every observed symptom (PTC peerless, envelope gossip
  failure, envelope HTTP 404, register_validator 500). The single strongest signal is Nimbus publishing payload-attestations to a topic with zero subscribers — meaning no other client was listening on the topic
  ID Nimbus computed.

  Next step

  Verify that every CL client computes the same topic ID for:
  - /eth2/<fork_digest>/execution_payload_envelope/ssz_snappy
  - /eth2/<fork_digest>/payload_attestation_message/ssz_snappy

  If those IDs disagree, that is the defect. Recovery requires restarting from a snapshot before slot 22688.

```

author: 0xunclebill
content:
nah this doesn’t sound right, the no peers thing is because of peering issues (peers banning each other) not because of topic id disagreement 

Payload ID unavailable is potentially an EL issue.

author: 0xunclebill
content: I started digging into the logs today I’ll try to spend more time tomorrow looking through it as well

content: Wouldn't this have triggered failures well before this? The network was running at literally 100% for days
